A guided tutorial to use Seurcery¶

In this tutorial we use a processed public PBMC3K dataset to test the usage of Seurcery. The raw dataset can be downloaded here.

Usage of Seurat::VlnPlot¶

In [1]:
library(Seurat)
library(ggplot2)

pbmc <- readRDS("PBMC3k_Test.rds")

options(repr.plot.width = 8, repr.plot.height = 6)
VlnPlot(pbmc, features = "CD8A", pt.size = 0)

Attaching SeuratObject

Seurat v4 was just loaded with SeuratObject v5; disabling v5 assays and
validation routines, and ensuring assays work in strict v3/v4
compatibility mode

Warning message:
“The `slot` argument of `FetchData()` is deprecated as of SeuratObject 5.0.0.
ℹ Please use the `layer` argument instead.
ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the Seurat package.
  Please report the issue at <https://github.com/satijalab/seurat/issues>.”
Warning message:
“`PackageCheck()` was deprecated in SeuratObject 5.0.0.
ℹ Please use `rlang::check_installed()` instead.
ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the Seurat package.
  Please report the issue at <https://github.com/satijalab/seurat/issues>.”

Import Seurcery¶

In [2]:
library(Seurcery)
packageVersion("Seurcery")

[1] ‘0.8.5’

Basic usage of geom_vln_gradient()¶

Visualize by default

In [3]:
VlnPlot(pbmc, features = "CD8A", pt.size = 0) + geom_vln_gradient()

Style adjustments with outline and outline_size

In [4]:
VlnPlot(pbmc, features = "CD8A", pt.size = 0) + geom_vln_gradient(outline = F)
VlnPlot(pbmc, features = "CD8A", pt.size = 0) + geom_vln_gradient(outline_size = 1)

Color quantile adjustment with low_quantile, mid_quantile, and high_quantile

In [5]:
VlnPlot(pbmc, features = "CD8A", pt.size = 0) + geom_vln_gradient(low_quantile = 0.5, mid_quantile = 0.9)

Visualize by reversed gradient colors with direction = -1

In [6]:
VlnPlot(pbmc, features = "CD8A", pt.size = 0) + geom_vln_gradient(direction = -1)

Visualize multiple genes

In [7]:
VlnPlot(pbmc, features = c("CD8A", "GZMK", "CCL5", "S100A4"), pt.size = 0, ncol = 2) + geom_vln_gradient()

Basic usage of geom_dot_gradient()¶

The default color of Seurcery::geom_dot_gradient() is gray, reflecting the average expression level in the groups through transparency. Color can be added via the group_cols parameter if needed.

In [8]:
options(repr.plot.width = 14, repr.plot.height = 6)
In [9]:
VlnPlot(pbmc, features = "CD8A", pt.size = 0) + geom_dot_gradient()

Scale for y is already present.
Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

One-sided significance test with sig_test_all (default: Wilcox test)

In [10]:
VlnPlot(pbmc, features = "CD8A", pt.size = 0) + geom_dot_gradient(sig_test_all = "wilcox")

Scale for y is already present.
Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.

Visualize multiple genes

In [11]:
options(repr.plot.width = 16, repr.plot.height = 12)
VlnPlot(pbmc, features = c("CD8A", "GZMK", "CCL5", "S100A4"), pt.size = 0, ncol = 2) + 
    geom_dot_gradient(sig_test_all = "wilcox")

Scale for y is already present.
Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.
Scale for y is already present.
Adding another scale for y, which will replace the existing scale.
